expectations.text: Adds kelet to pyproject.toml or requirements
expectations.evidence: Transcript notes kelet>=1.3.0 was already in pyproject.toml; skill correctly detected and skipped install step. Dependency satisfied.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() is called at startup (not per-request)
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.configure()` added at module level in app/main.py (line 19), outside any request handler.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps the agent call with the existing Redis session_id
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id):` wrapping `chat_agent.iter()` in src/routers/chat.py.

expectations.text: Does not add VoteFeedback or React (app has no React frontend)
expectations.evidence: Transcript explicitly notes 'No React frontend → no VoteFeedback, no useFeedbackState'. Signal analysis confirms 0 coded signals. No React or VoteFeedback references in diff.

expectations.text: KELET_API_KEY and KELET_PROJECT written to .env (not hardcoded in source)
expectations.evidence: Transcript lines 164-166 confirm .env updated with KELET_PROJECT=docs-ai-assistant and KELET_API_KEY=sk-kelet-test-123. settings/__init__.py had kelet_api_key/kelet_project fields removed (SDK reads env vars directly). Keys not hardcoded in source.

expectations.text: AskUserQuestion called at most 3 times total
expectations.evidence: questions_asked.md shows exactly 2 AskUserQuestion calls (Checkpoint 1 and Checkpoint 2). Well within the 3-max budget.

expectations.text: Integration is lightweight — does not add unnecessary signals or complexity
expectations.evidence: Transcript: 'Coded signals: 0 (lightweight, no hooks to wire to)'. Changes limited to configure(), agentic_session(), and env vars. No unnecessary decorators, signal calls, or extra dependencies added.

expectations.text: Synthetic evaluator deeplink is generated via Bash execution (not shown as copy-paste code block)
expectations.evidence: deeplink.txt contains a real https://console.kelet.ai/docs-ai-assistant/synthetics/setup?deeplink=<base64url> URL. Transcript states 'Deeplink generated (Bash)' and shows the URL directly (not as a code block). deeplink.txt confirms actual execution.
